title: Works in Progress: Grid Connection Auctions - Marginal REVOLUTION
gist: Tabarrok summarizes a Works in Progress piece by Chris Gillett: US grid bottleneck is interconnection, not generation; first-come-first-served queues misallocate, auctions and flexible loads are the fix, and market prices have already steered ERCOT toward solar then storage.

insights.claim: The US has ample electricity generation capacity ready to deploy; the binding constraint is connecting it to the grid, not building more generation.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: Reframes the energy buildout problem as a queue/regulatory issue — points investors toward interconnection reform and transmission rather than pure generation plays.
insights.quote: the US has plenty of electricity generation capacity ready to go, the problem is connecting it to the grid
insights.locator: Chris Gillett points out something surprising

insights.claim: The grid's first-come-first-served interconnection queue lets low-priority requests block high-priority ones for years, analogous to small Cessnas bumping 747s at a congested airport.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: Identifies a specific allocation failure with a known economic fix — a policy edge to watch for in grid reform.
insights.quote: we have an airport congestion problem in which small Cessnas can bump 747s
insights.locator: The main flaw of the interconnection process

insights.claim: Auctions for connection rights are the solution to the interconnection queue, and flexible loads like data centers that can disconnect during peak hours should not have to wait years for firm service.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: Flexible interconnection could fast-track data-center power access — directly relevant to AI compute buildout timelines.
insights.quote: if a data center can disconnect or use backup power during the few peak hours each year, it should not have to wait years for firm service
insights.locator: Gillett also emphasizes that some loads should be allowed to connect on a flexible basis

insights.claim: As ERCOT solar output rose from 4 to 29.8 GW (Dec 2020-Sep 2025), the value of power at 1pm relative to the peak hour fell from 92.9% to 38.7%, showing prices reprice midday power as solar is overbuilt.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: Concrete evidence of solar cannibalizing its own midday value — a price signal investors can read to time storage and dispatchable-capacity bets.
insights.quote: the value of power at 1pm relative to the highest-priced hour decreased from 92.9 percent to 38.7 percent
insights.locator: From December 2020 to September 2025, maximum solar output in ERCOT

insights.claim: Responding to the solar-driven price shape, ERCOT went from almost no battery storage to 8.6 GW of combined battery discharge between 2020 and October 2025.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: Demonstrates the rapid storage buildout that follows solar saturation — a repeatable pattern across markets investors can front-run.
insights.quote: ERCOT went from having almost no battery storage to a combined battery discharge of 8.6 gigawatts
insights.locator: The evolving daily price shape in response to the abundance of solar energy
